sections.paragraph:
ἀπειλέω (s. ἀπειλή) impf. ἠπείλουν; fut. 3 sg. ἀπειλήσει Is 66:14; 1 aor. mid. ἠπειλησάμην (on the mid. cp. B-D-F §316, 1); pass. inf. ἀπειληθῆναι LXX (Hom. et al.; pap, LXX; Jos., Ant. 5, 144; Test12Patr, Just.) threaten, warn τινί someone, foll. by μή and inf. warn (them) no longer to Ac 4:17 (v.l. adds ἀπειλῇ). τὶ (4 Macc 9:5; Jos., Ant. 13, 143 πόλεμον) with someth. πῦρ MPol 11:2; cp. Just., A I, 2, 1 θάνατος. Abs., of Christ πάσχων οὐκ ἠπείλει although he suffered he did not threaten 1 Pt 2:23.—B. 1279. M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

inf. inf. = infinitive

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

foll. foll. = followed, following

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

someth. someth. = something

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 100
